meaning: An official who makes sure that players follow the rules of a game.
example: The referee blew his whistle to stop the soccer match after a foul.

meaning: A punishment given to a player or team for breaking a rule.
example: The team received a penalty because one of their players touched the ball with their hands.

meaning: An unfair play or action in a game that goes against the rules.
example: Pushing another player is a foul and will result in a free kick.

meaning: A position in sports like soccer or hockey where a player is in an illegal spot, usually too close to the opponent's goal.
example: The striker scored a goal, but it did not count because he was offside.

meaning: Extra time played at the end of a game when the score is tied.
example: The basketball game went into overtime after both teams scored 90 points.

meaning: A series of games or contests in which many players or teams compete to win a final championship.
example: The tennis tournament lasts for two weeks and has over a hundred players.

meaning: A player who replaces another player during a game.
example: The coach decided to send in a substitute player in the second half of the match.

meaning: To stop someone from playing or competing in a game because they did not follow the rules.
example: The runner was disqualified for starting the race before the whistle blew.

meaning: Outside the playing area of a field or court.
example: The tennis ball landed out of bounds, so the point went to the other player.

meaning: The part of a team that tries to stop the other team from scoring points.
example: Our team has a strong defense, so it is difficult for opponents to score.

meaning: The part of a team that tries to score goals or points.
example: The team's offense is very fast and has scored many goals this season.

meaning: A game that ends with the same score for both teams; a tie.
example: The match ended in a 1-1 draw, so both teams received one point.

meaning: The final game or match to decide who is the best player or team in a sport.
example: They trained hard all year to win the state soccer championship.

meaning: A short rest period between the two halves of a sports game.
example: During halftime, the players drank water and listened to the coach's new plan.

meaning: A series of extra games played at the end of a regular season to decide the champion.
example: Only the top eight teams in the league qualify for the playoffs.

meaning: A person who watches a sports event or game.
example: Thousands of spectators filled the stadium to watch the final race.

meaning: A person who trains and guides athletes or teams.
example: The coach gave the team instructions on how to improve their passing.

meaning: The chosen leader of a sports team.
example: The captain shook hands with the referee before the game started.

meaning: Fair, polite, and honest behavior when playing a sport or game.
example: Shaking hands with your opponent after losing is a sign of good sportsmanship.

meaning: To lose a game automatically because you cannot play or because you broke a rule.
example: The team had to forfeit the game because they did not have enough players.

meaning: A player or team that is expected to lose a game or competition.
example: Everyone was surprised when the underdog team won the final match.

meaning: An abbreviation for Most Valuable Player, an award given to the best player in a game or season.
example: Sarah was named MVP after scoring three goals in the final match.

meaning: The ranking of players or teams in a tournament based on their past performance.
example: Because she has the highest seeding, she will play against lower-ranked players first.

meaning: A flat frame used for carrying an injured player off the field.
example: The player injured his leg and had to be carried away on a stretcher.

meaning: The sports field or stadium that belongs to a specific team.
example: Our team usually plays better when we are on our home turf.

meaning: The ability to keep doing a difficult physical activity for a long time.
example: Running a marathon requires a high level of physical endurance.

meaning: The physical or mental strength to continue an activity without getting tired.
example: Swimming is a great way to build up your stamina.

meaning: The process of drinking enough water to keep your body healthy and working well.
example: Proper hydration is very important when you exercise in hot weather.

meaning: Gentle exercises you do before a workout to prepare your muscles.
example: A ten-minute warm-up helps prevent injuries during heavy lifting.

meaning: Slow and easy exercises you do at the end of a workout to help your body relax.
example: Walking slowly on the treadmill is a good way to cool down.

meaning: Doing a single movement or exercise one time (often called a 'rep').
example: Try to complete ten repetitions of this exercise in each set.

meaning: A group of repetitions of an exercise done without stopping.
example: He completed three sets of fifteen push-ups.

meaning: A short bar with weights at each end, held in one hand for exercise.
example: She picked up a light dumbbell to exercise her arm muscles.

meaning: A long metal bar with weights on both ends, held with two hands.
example: He loaded weights onto the barbell before starting his squats.

meaning: An exercise machine with a moving belt that you can walk or run on in one place.
example: It was raining outside, so I ran for three miles on the treadmill.

meaning: Exercises that use only your own body weight to build strength and fitness.
example: Push-ups, pull-ups, and squats are common examples of calisthenics.

meaning: The time your body needs to rest and repair muscles after hard exercise.
example: Adequate sleep and good food are essential for muscle recovery.

meaning: An injury caused by twisting a joint, like an ankle or wrist, which causes pain and swelling.
example: She tripped during the race and suffered a painful ankle sprain.

meaning: The speed at which your heart beats, measured in beats per minute.
example: You can use a smartwatch to check your heart rate while running.

meaning: Exercises that strengthen the heart and lungs, like running or cycling.
example: I do thirty minutes of cardio three times a week to stay healthy.

meaning: The ability of joints and muscles to bend and move easily through their full range.
example: Yoga is excellent for improving your balance and flexibility.

meaning: Exercising too much or too hard without enough rest, leading to fatigue or injury.
example: Overtraining can make you feel tired and reduce your athletic performance.

meaning: An elastic band used in strength training to make exercises harder.
example: She wrapped the resistance band around her legs to do squats.

meaning: A person who watches and helps someone who is lifting weights to keep them safe.
example: Can you act as my spotter while I lift this heavy barbell?

meaning: A dangerous condition caused by losing too much water from the body.
example: Headache and dizziness are common signs of dehydration.

meaning: The process by which your body converts food and drink into energy.
example: Regular weight training can help speed up your metabolism.

meaning: A substance in food like meat, eggs, and beans that helps build and repair muscles.
example: Drinking a protein shake after a workout can help your muscles heal faster.

meaning: Energetic physical exercises done to music, designed to improve fitness.
example: My grandmother attends an aerobics class at the local community center.

meaning: The muscles around your stomach, back, and hips that keep your body stable.
example: Planks are one of the best exercises to strengthen your core.

meaning: Physical damage or harm done to a person's body during sports or exercise.
example: Stretching before you run can help reduce the risk of a muscle injury.

meaning: The ability to move quickly and change direction easily.
example: The soccer player showed great agility by dribbling past three defenders.

meaning: The ability to remain steady and not fall over while standing or moving.
example: Gymnastics helps children develop a good sense of balance.

meaning: The ability to use different parts of the body together smoothly and efficiently.
example: Playing tennis requires excellent hand-eye coordination.

meaning: The speed at which you run, walk, or move.
example: You need to set a steady pace at the start of the race so you do not get tired too quickly.

meaning: A single long step taken when running or walking.
example: The runner had a long, smooth stride that helped him cover distance quickly.

meaning: A person who is trained or skilled in exercises, sports, or games.
example: The Olympic athlete trained for eight hours every day.

meaning: Strong, fit, and active; physically good at sports.
example: He has a very athletic build because he plays basketball and runs regularly.

meaning: How well a person plays, runs, or does an activity.
example: The team's performance in the second half was much better than in the first.

meaning: A natural ability to do something well, without training.
example: She has a natural talent for gymnastics and learned the moves very quickly.

meaning: A particular way of doing an activity that requires skill or practice.
example: The coach helped him correct his swimming technique to move faster through the water.

meaning: A long-distance running race that is exactly 26.2 miles (42.195 kilometers) long.
example: He trained for six months to run his first marathon.

meaning: To run as fast as you can over a short distance.
example: The runners sprinted the last 50 meters to cross the finish line.

meaning: A series of frames or barriers that runners must jump over in a race.
example: She won the 100-meter hurdles race with a record time.

meaning: A race in which team members take turns running parts of the total distance.
example: The relay team won gold after a perfect baton pass.

meaning: A light spear thrown for distance in a track and field competition.
example: He threw the javelin further than anyone else in the school competition.

meaning: An athletic event consisting of ten different track and field contests.
example: The decathlon is a true test of an athlete's speed, strength, and skills.

meaning: A collection of sports that involve running, jumping, and throwing events.
example: Track and field events are a major highlight of the Olympic Games.

meaning: A sport where you travel across water in a small, narrow boat using a double-bladed paddle.
example: We went kayaking on the river and saw beautiful birds along the shore.

meaning: The sport or skill of shooting arrows with a bow at a target.
example: You need a steady hand and good concentration to succeed in archery.

meaning: The sport or activity of riding a bicycle.
example: Cycling is not only a great sport but also an eco-friendly way to travel.

meaning: The sport of climbing up natural rock walls or indoor artificial walls.
example: Rock climbing requires strong fingers and a lot of upper body strength.

meaning: Walking a long distance over several days, usually in mountains or wild areas.
example: They spent a week trekking through the Himalayas with their heavy backpacks.

meaning: The sport of climbing mountains, often involving ice, snow, and ropes.
example: Mountaineering is an exciting but dangerous sport that requires special training.

meaning: Swimming near the surface of the water while using a tube to breathe and a mask to see.
example: During our tropical holiday, we enjoyed snorkeling near the coral reef.

meaning: Relating to horse riding or sports involving horses.
example: The equestrian competition includes events like jumping and dressage.

meaning: A continuous race consisting of three different sports: swimming, cycling, and running.
example: She finished the triathlon in under three hours, which was an amazing result.

meaning: A type of rock climbing done on low walls or large rocks without ropes or harnesses.
example: Bouldering is very popular because you only need climbing shoes and a chalk bag.

meaning: Walking long distances in nature, such as on paths or trails in the forest.
example: We went hiking in the national park to see the beautiful waterfalls.

meaning: The sport of moving a boat through water using long wooden oars.
example: The university rowing team practices early in the morning on the lake.

meaning: A sport in which you jump from a high place and float down using a special wide parachute.
example: He experienced a rush of adrenaline while paragliding over the valley.

meaning: The sport of riding on a board on the face of a wave.
example: He loves surfing at the beach during the summer months.

meaning: The sport of traveling down a fast-flowing river on an inflatable raft.
example: Whitewater rafting is a thrilling outdoor sport that requires teamwork.

meaning: An outdoor game in which players use special clubs to hit a small ball into a series of holes in as few strokes as possible.
example: He plays golf with his friends every Saturday morning at the local course.

meaning: An outdoor game played with a bat and ball by two teams of eleven players on a field with a pitch in the middle.
example: Cricket is a very popular sport in India, England, and Australia.

meaning: A game played with a bat and ball by two teams of nine players on a field with four bases.
example: He hit a home run during yesterday's baseball game.

meaning: A special computer system designed specifically for playing video games on a television or screen.
example: He bought the latest video game console on the day it was released.

meaning: A handheld device used to control characters or actions in a video game.
example: Pass me the other controller so we can play together.

meaning: The specific way a video game is played, including its rules, activities, and experience.
example: The graphics of this game are simple, but the gameplay is very fun and exciting.

meaning: A digital character that represents a player in a virtual world or video game.
example: You can change the clothes and hairstyle of your avatar in the game.

meaning: To reach a higher stage or level in a video game by earning points or completing tasks.
example: You need to defeat this monster to level up and get new abilities.

meaning: A mission or task that a player must complete in a game to earn rewards.
example: The main quest of the game is to find the hidden treasure and save the town.

meaning: A video game designed for more than one person to play at the same time.
example: We played a multiplayer game online with friends from different countries.

meaning: A video game designed to be played by only one person at a time.
example: I prefer single-player games because I can enjoy the story at my own pace.

meaning: To appear back in the game after your character dies or is defeated.
example: When you die in this game, you respawn at the nearest campfire.

meaning: A temporary increase in a character's power, speed, or abilities in a video game.
example: The magic potion gave my character a temporary speed buff.

meaning: To make a character, weapon, or ability weaker in a video game update to keep the game fair.
example: The game developers decided to nerf the sword because it was too powerful.

meaning: The minimum time a player must wait before using an ability or weapon again in a game.
example: I couldn't use my healing spell because it was still on cooldown.

meaning: The main story mode of a video game, divided into different levels or missions.
example: It took me twenty hours of gameplay to complete the game's campaign.

meaning: A type of video game that gives players a lot of freedom to create, build, or explore without strict goals.
example: Minecraft is a famous sandbox game where you can build anything you want.

meaning: A video game that tries to copy real-life activities, like driving a plane or managing a city.
example: He bought a flight simulation game to practice landing airplanes.

meaning: The act of completing a video game as fast as possible.
example: He watched a speedrun of his favorite childhood game on the internet.

meaning: Items, weapons, or money that players collect in a video game.
example: Defeating the dragon allows players to collect rare and valuable loot.

meaning: A small payment made online to buy virtual items or extra content within a video game.
example: The game is free to download, but it contains many microtransactions for special outfits.

meaning: An online system in video games that pairs players of similar skill levels to play together.
example: The game's matchmaking system took three minutes to find an opponent for me.

meaning: A character in a video game that is controlled by the computer, not by a player (NPC).
example: The non-player character in the shop gave us a new quest.

meaning: A small, temporary programming error or bug in a video game.
example: A strange glitch made my character float in the air for a few seconds.

meaning: The highest points ever recorded in a particular game.
example: She was proud to see her name at the top of the high score board.

meaning: Organized, competitive video gaming events where professional players compete against each other.
example: Esports tournaments now attract millions of viewers worldwide.

meaning: A delay or slow response in an online game, usually caused by a poor internet connection.
example: I lost the match because severe lag made it impossible to control my character.

meaning: The visual images and designs shown on the screen during a video game.
example: Modern video games have incredibly realistic 3D graphics.

meaning: A winning position in chess where the opponent's king cannot escape capture.
example: He won the game of chess by putting my king in checkmate.

meaning: A square board divided into 64 alternating black and white squares, used to play chess.
example: Set up the pieces on the chessboard so we can start our game.

meaning: Small plastic cubes with dots representing numbers 1 to 6 on each side, used in games.
example: You need to roll the dice to find out how many spaces your token can move.

meaning: A game played on a marked board, usually involving pieces or cards.
example: Playing a board game with family is a fun activity on a rainy evening.

meaning: A careful plan of actions designed to achieve victory or a goal.
example: You need a good strategy if you want to win this complex board game.

meaning: The weakest and most common piece in the game of chess.
example: He sacrificed a pawn to protect his queen.

meaning: A complete set of playing cards, usually consisting of 52 cards.
example: He shuffled the card deck before dealing them to the players.

meaning: To trick or deceive opponents by pretending to have better cards or a stronger position than you actually do.
example: In poker, sometimes you have to bluff to win a round with weak cards.

meaning: To mix a deck of cards thoroughly so their order is random before starting a game.
example: Please shuffle the cards well before you hand them out.

meaning: The person who distributes the cards to the players in a card game.
example: The dealer handed five cards to each player at the table.

meaning: A game design where players take turns to make their moves one after the other.
example: Monopoly is a turn-based game, so you must wait for your friend to finish before you play.

meaning: A person who plays against you in a game or sport.
example: He shook hands with his opponent before the tennis match started.

meaning: An action of changing the position of a piece on a game board.
example: That was a very clever move that captured my knight.

meaning: A small object used to represent a player on a game board.
example: I chose the little silver car as my token for the board game.

meaning: To throw dice onto a surface to get a random number.
example: It is your turn to roll the dice and move your piece.

meaning: A small, flat piece used in games like Scrabble or Dominoes.
example: She picked up seven letters tiles from the bag to start the game.

meaning: A game or quiz testing players on facts and general knowledge.
example: We won first prize at the local pub's weekly trivia night.

meaning: A card game that is played by a single person.
example: He passed the time on the train by playing solitaire on his phone.

meaning: The stack of cards face down from which players take cards during a game.
example: If you do not have any playable cards, you must draw one from the draw pile.

meaning: The set of cards that a player holds in their hand during a game.
example: She had a very strong hand with two aces and a king.
